qa_pairs.question: What role does a group's supervisor play in enabling other groups to access files on the /project filesystem?
qa_pairs.answer: For the /project filesystem, a group's supervisor needs to set the appropriate Access Control Lists (ACLs) at the /project/G/GROUP level to allow users from other groups to access the files.

qa_pairs.question: Can I use Access Control Lists (ACLs) to transfer ownership or permissions of files or directories that I do not own?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Access Control Lists (ACLs) do not allow you to give away permissions to files or directories that do not belong to you.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended security practice for granting write permissions to other users on my /home directory on SciNet clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: It is strongly recommended never to grant write permission to other users on the top level of your /home directory (e.g., /home/G/GROUP/[owner]), as this can seriously compromise your privacy and disable SSH key authentication. Instead, create specific sub-directories under your home directory for other users to manipulate or access files.
